message:
Security hardening: ct_eq, domain separators, fail-closed boundaries across 22 source files

- ct_eq timing-attack fixes: 10 hash/digest comparisons in 8 files
  (state_model, anti_entropy, marker_stream, time_travel_engine,
  claim_compiler, proof_service, receipt_chain, deterministic_seed,
  migration_pipeline, rollback_bundle)
- Truncation cast: fleet_quarantine zone_count as u32 → try_from
- Domain separators: 4 SHA-256 hashes in main.rs + verifier_routes.rs
- Fail-closed boundaries: 13 sites in 8 files (> → >=, <= → <)
  (verification_state, checkpoint_guard, epoch_transition,
  snapshot_policy, category_shift, provenance, bpet_migration_gate,
  dgis_migration_gate)
- Updated 3 tests for new fail-closed boundary semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -576,7 +576,7 @@ pub fn rollback(mut state: PipelineState) -> Result<PipelineState, PipelineError
 pub fn is_idempotent(a: &PipelineState, b: &PipelineState) -> bool {
     a.current_stage == b.current_stage
         && a.cohort_id == b.cohort_id
-        && a.idempotency_key == b.idempotency_key
+        && crate::security::constant_time::ct_eq(&a.idempotency_key, &b.idempotency_key)
         && a.extensions == b.extensions
         && a.schema_version == b.schema_version
 }
